description:
Il Crostino Della Mamma

Creiamo una Millefoglie di pane carrè con salsa di pomodori Sammarzano, Mozzarella di Bufala DOP, Prosciutto cotto slow cooked, Funghi trifolati,
Carciofi, Wrustel, Spianata Calabra e Olive.
La Tosteremo e ve la serviremo fumante.

“Let's create a Millefeuille of carrè bread with Sammarzano tomato sauce, Mozzarella di Bufala DOP, Slow cooked ham, Sautéed mushrooms,

Artichokes, Wrustel, Calabrian Esplanade and Olives.

We will toast it and serve it to you steaming.”

Which way to go?
description: Unexpected flooding in Venice in June 2016. Some people were prepared, some others were not. Some locals had rubber boots, tourists usually bought the 15 Euro plastic boots and others improvised trash bag boots or were ready to walk through the nasty canal water barefooted. Stores like the one in the photo below had dated water marks on their door frames. The flooding, which usually happened once a year in winter, happens more often today. Global warming!

title: Carneval mask
description:
The Carnival of Venice (Italian: Carnevale di Venezia) is an annual festival that ends with the Christian celebration of Lent, forty days before Easter, on Shrove Tuesday (Martedì Grasso or Mardi Gras), the day before Ash Wednesday. The festival is world-famous for its elaborate masks.

The redevelopment of the masks in 1979 began as the pursuit of some Venetian college students for the tourist trade. Since then, approximately 3 million visitors come to Venice every year for the Carnival. One of the most important events is the contest for la maschera più bella ("the most beautiful mask") which is judged by a panel of international costume and fashion designers. (Wikipedia)

title: Phot.Venice.Trattoria.Boss.041024.4802.jpg
description:
Italy, Venice, …the "big" boss of the "Trattoria-Pizzeria Conca d'Oro".

It is said that here, near the San Marcos square, Venice's first pizza was baked in a charcoal oven in 1947. The Trattoria-Pizzeria was founded by the Sicilian brothers Giuseppe & Giovanni Costa, a story that is still ongoing today. 

The lagoon city hides many picturesque places offside the tourist roads. If the buildings would be straight, painted & well maintained, I think Venice without this patina, the gondolas & gondolieri, channels, little bridges & the at all-time busy Canal Grande; all these main ingredients altogether express the romantic charm of Venice.  

👉 One World one Dream,
🙏...Danke, Xièxie 谢谢, Thanks, Gracias, Merci, Grazie, Obrigado, Arigatô, Dhanyavad, Chokrane to you & over

title: Phot.Venice.Trattoria.Boss.041024.4800.jpg
tags: italy venice nikon d800 d300 d200 f2 f3 f4 coolpix frankartculinaryyahoode italien italia venedig venezia venise murano burano square places place plaza plätze strasen rue calle strada streets historic market gondolas gondoliere yacht sailing piazza sanmarco gondeln gondole góndola voga canalgrande rialto cruiseliner
description:
Italy, Venice, …the "big" boss of the "Trattoria-Pizzeria Conca d'Oro".

It is said that here, near the San Marcos square, Venice's first pizza was baked in a charcoal oven in 1947. The Trattoria-Pizzeria was founded by the Sicilian brothers Giuseppe & Giovanni Costa, a story that is still ongoing today. 

The lagoon city hides many picturesque places offside the tourist roads. If the buildings would be straight, painted & well maintained, I think Venice without this patina, the gondolas & gondolieri, channels, little bridges & the at all-time busy Canal Grande; all these main ingredients altogether express the romantic charm of Venice.  

👉 One World one Dream,
🙏...Danke, Xièxie 谢谢, Thanks, Gracias, Merci, Grazie, Obrigado, Arigatô, Dhanyavad, Chokrane to you & over 
13  million visits in my photostream with countless motivating comments

title: Gondola after dark #10
description:
Venice, Italy.

Carolyn and I were on a cruise which stopped in Venice. The tour offered an optional "after dark" gondola ride. 

Tips for future travelers: 
1. Take your ride in the daytime. It is just as "romantic", you can actually see, and you don't dwell on the smells on the lesser, still water canals.
2. Arrange your own ride. The price will easily be less for a private ride as opposed to what you pay for an "arranged" ride with at least one more couple.

title: Flan di pistacchi, semifreddo e gelato di pistacchi
tags: venice dinner yum january pistachio 2010 60225mm ilridotto
description:
I wasn't a huge fan of the one on the left, but the other two were great. 

Poor, neglected cookies off in the distance.  =)

title: Cookies!
description: So this was the "complementary dessert" portion of the evening. In hindsight I feel really bad that I didn't try any of the cookies since I'd also ordered another dessert, but how was I to know? I was more stuffed than the squid! =p I have absolutely no clue what that liqueur was, but it was potent.

title: Calamaro ripieno di scampi, gamberi, carote e zucchine, in guazzetto di vongole veraci
tags: venice dinner yum january squid 2010 60225mm ilridotto
description: Surprising absolutely no one, I ordered the stuffed squid -- which was, well -- let's go with "amazing."

title: Risotto alle erbe fini di S. Erasmo in fumetto di pesci e crostacei
description:
I'm not a risotto expert, but this was really, really good.

Once again, this was a half portion, so don't go freaking about Venetian portions and prices;  Gianni doesn't skimp.
comments: moppet65535: And it comes with a double helping of bowl. | bsii: For the record, the "full portion" was served on a plate.  Wait, don't you know the inverse square law of risotto presentation?

title: Ravioli di gamberi, essenza di pomodoro e ricotta fresca
description:
The risotto was served to me first, but just as I'd touched my fork to the dish he came with my other primi and pulled the risotto away, letting me know the ravioli were better experienced first.

He was right.  

These were amazing, with a nice light freshness -- not mentioned in the description is the orange zest or something that was used in the sauce, really adding to the depth.  If anything was a let down here, it was probably the prawns themselves -- they were a little flavorless and the texture didn't mesh with the rest of the dish.
comments: queendork: This all looks amazing. | bsii: It didn't suck. ;-) Barring the prawns in the ravioli, I think this was my favorite dish -- either that or the amuse bouche. But previously unmentioned, both the cheese and the tomatoes here were also great. The total for what amounted to 4 courses -- along with the amuse bouche and the cookies/liqueur, *and* two glasses of wine -- was admittedly €70. But, between it being Venice, it being super great, and the implicit experience of the super involved chef providing great service and catering to my two half primis, I actually don't think that was outrageous. Besides, seriously, I live in Dublin. ;-p | bsii: And as a point of comparison, the octopus and cauliflower side and a glass of wine (and maybe I paid for the water and the bread) came to something like €35. It wasn't terrible, but let's just say that that wasn't the sweet spot on Venice's value curve.

title: Amuse bouche
tags: venice dinner yum january 2010 amusebouche 60225mm ilridotto
description:
Some warm fish with some salt and olive oil, in... I have no idea what the purple stuff was -- almost a yogurt... berry maybe? -- but the entire bite was pretty amazing.  (And complementary!)

It was also rad to see the chef peaking out from the kitchen to see how we reacted to it; I hope "thumbs up" isn't a rude gesture in Venice.

title: Il Ridotto's hors d'oeuvres
tags: venice dinner menu yum january 2010 60225mm ilridotto
description: I didn't get anything from here, though I was tempted by the lobster salad or the octopus.

title: Il Ridotto's desserts
tags: venice dinner menu yum january 2010 60225mm ilridotto
description: This one would probably stump Rick and Trisha, *first* in that I even ordered a dessert, and second, that it wasn't tiramisu.  Knowing those two items, however, would probably lead them to correctly guess that I got the pistachio trio.

title: Il Ridotto's other 2.5  tables
description: There was one more table for two to my left, and that was it;  the kitchen was around the corner to the left, beyond the cabinent.

title: Il Ridotto's secondis
tags: venice dinner menu yum january 2010 60225mm ilridotto
description: There wasn't even a little bit of question about which dish I was going to order here.  Well, maybe a *little*, but I once again suspect both Rick and Trisha could get it in one.

title: Il Ridotto's primis
tags: venice dinner menu yum january 2010 60225mm ilridotto
description:
I couldn't make up my mind between the ravioli and the risotto;  I asked the chef and owner who was also doing the table service and telling us about the food and generally being awesome for a recommendation, and he said that they were both very different;  he said that since "the lady" had also ordered the risotto, he could make me a half portion of that and the ravioli.

It was €25 for both.  Rad.  =)

title: Anchovy and egg.
description:
Cost something like €0.20 for this quick bite.

PS.  I love food.  =)
